question_text: A rechargeable battery stores 7200 J of chemical energy. When used to power a motor for 1 hour, the motor converts 80% of the chemical energy into kinetic energy and dissipates the rest as thermal energy. How much thermal energy is wasted, and what does this tell you about real energy conversions?
correct_answer: 1440 J of thermal energy is wasted (20% of 7200 J). This shows that real energy conversions are never 100% efficient; some energy always dissipates as heat due to friction and other irreversible processes.
explanation: Thermal energy wasted = 20% of 7200 J = 0.2 × 7200 = 1440 J. Perfect energy conversion is impossible in real systems because friction, air resistance, and mechanical inefficiencies always cause some energy to transfer to less useful thermal stores.
